title: Hieracium neolygodes
intro: Hieracium neolygodes, also known as a hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits in treating wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium neolygodes include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium neolygodes is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its therapeutic benefits.
